description: You already build REST APIs (DRF, dynamo's SSE endpoint), so REST goes straight to deep/production topics. GraphQL and gRPC are genuinely new — gRPC especially matters for high-performance inference/agent communication. Rounded out with API security and tooling.

topics.title: REST (deep)
topics.note: You already build REST APIs — this fills in the design-maturity gaps, not the basics.

subtopics.title: Richardson Maturity Model & HATEOAS — why most 'REST' APIs stop at level 2, and when level 3 is worth it
Idempotency keys for POST/PATCH (critical for retryable inference/training-trigger requests)
Proper HTTP status code usage & error response standardization (RFC 7807 Problem Details)
Content negotiation, conditional requests (ETag/If-None-Match) for caching large model outputs
Designing REST for streaming-adjacent use cases: your SSE endpoint as a 'REST + streaming hybrid' — documenting it properly (OpenAPI extensions)
resources: Self-directed: write an OpenAPI spec for the dynamo API

topics.title: GraphQL (optional but powerful)
topics.note: Marked optional per your own framing — enough here to know when it beats REST, not full mastery.

subtopics.title: GraphQL fundamentals: schema, queries/mutations/subscriptions, resolvers
Subscriptions for real-time data (relevant overlap with your WebSocket/agent-dashboard use case)
Strawberry/Graphene for adding a GraphQL layer on top of Django models
REST vs GraphQL tradeoffs for your actual use cases (agent dashboards with nested/relational data vs simple inference calls)

topics.title: gRPC (for high-performance systems)
topics.note: New but high-value — this is what real ML-serving infra (TorchServe, Triton) and multi-agent systems often use internally.

subtopics.title: Protocol Buffers: defining .proto schemas, code generation
gRPC fundamentals: unary vs streaming RPCs (server streaming maps directly onto your token-streaming use case)
Building a gRPC service for dynamo inference — compare latency/overhead vs your SSE/HTTP implementation
gRPC for internal agent-to-agent communication in your multi-agent coding tool (faster than HTTP+JSON for tool calls)
resources: Self-directed, ties into your Groq multi-agent project

subtopics.title: gRPC-Web / gateway patterns when a browser frontend needs to talk to a gRPC backend

topics.title: API Security (rate limiting, auth, API gateways)
topics.note: Auth (JWT/OAuth) already covered in Backend Engineering — this focuses on the API-layer-specific security concerns.

subtopics.title: Rate limiting algorithms: token bucket, sliding window, fixed window — implement at least one from scratch
resources: Cloudflare blog: 'How we built rate limiting'

subtopics.title: API gateway patterns: Kong/Nginx/Traefik for centralized auth, rate limiting, routing across microservices
resources: Kong Gateway docs (or Traefik for a lighter-weight option)

subtopics.title: Input validation & injection prevention specific to APIs (mass assignment, over-fetching, GraphQL query depth limits)
API keys & scoped access tokens for external/agent consumers (different trust levels than end-user JWTs)
resources: Stripe/GitHub API key design as reference

subtopics.title: Securing your dynamo inference endpoint against abuse: per-key rate limits, request size limits, prompt-length caps
resources: Self-directed hardening exercise on your own API

subtopics.title: Postman/Thunder Client fundamentals: collections, environments, chained requests
Automated API testing/collection scripts (Postman test scripts or Thunder Client equivalent) wired into CI
OpenAPI/Swagger spec generation from DRF/FastAPI and using it to auto-generate a Postman collection
Versioning strategies: URI versioning vs header versioning vs no versioning (evolve-in-place) — pick one for the dynamo API
Deprecation strategy: sunset headers, changelogs, backward-compat shims

topics.title: Goal Projects: Production-grade, secure, multi-protocol API layer

subtopics.title: Write a full OpenAPI spec for the dynamo API and auto-generate docs + a Postman collection
Add gRPC as an alternative high-performance interface to dynamo inference alongside the existing REST/SSE endpoint
Harden the API layer: rate limiting, API keys for external consumers, and a minimal gateway (Traefik) in front of Django/FastAPI
Decide and document a versioning strategy for dynamo's API as it evolves (new model versions, new endpoints)
